question: What are the primary advantages of using Solid-State Drives (SSDs) over traditional Hard Disk Drives (HDDs) in a computer system?
expectedPoints: Faster access times and data transfer rates due to no moving parts.
Lower power consumption and heat generation.

question: Describe the purpose and process of disk scheduling. Compare and contrast the SSTF and SCAN disk scheduling algorithms, highlighting a key advantage and disadvantage of each.
expectedPoints: Purpose: To reduce the total head movement (seek time) and improve average response time for I/O requests.
SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First): Selects the request closest to the current head position. Advantage: Reduces average seek time. Disadvantage: Can cause starvation for requests in high-traffic areas.

SCAN (Elevator Algorithm): Moves the head in one direction, servicing all requests, until the end of the disk, then reverses direction. Advantage: Provides more uniform wait times. Disadvantage: Longer wait for requests just visited by the head.

question: Explain the concept of RAID and detail the specific characteristics, including performance, fault tolerance, and storage efficiency, for RAID levels 0, 1, and 5.
expectedPoints: RAID (Redundant Array of Independent Disks): Technology that combines multiple physical disks into a single logical unit for data redundancy, performance, or both.
RAID 0 (Striping): Data is split across disks. Characteristics: High performance (parallel I/O), no fault tolerance, 100% storage efficiency.
RAID 1 (Mirroring): Data is duplicated on a second disk. Characteristics: Good read performance, excellent fault tolerance (can lose one disk), 50% storage efficiency.

RAID 5 (Block-level striping with distributed parity): Data and parity are striped across all disks. Characteristics: Good read performance, good write performance (with parity calculation overhead), fault tolerance (can lose one disk), storage efficiency = (n-1)/n.
